requirement.original: /redev 真正影响你需求的（2 项，都跟“harness 别再骗你”直接相关）： 1. QA `manifest_verification` 用空 active 集刷绿 —— 这是你反复警告过的“做了个寂寞”模式的结构性漏洞。没有守卫，下一次 QA 同样能这么干。本次只修了这一次的报告，没修 agent 行为。下一周期加守卫（active_tests_count==0 时禁止 pytest_collected_ok:true，或者强制把 cycle-diff 文件加入 active 集）。 2. venv 全 broken symlink —— 我跑 pytest 时撞到要绕路。很可能就是 QA grep-only 验证的根因：subagent 在 venv 里跑 pytest 会失败，于是它退化成 grep。修这个 = 同时可能消除 #1 的诱因。
requirement.clarified: Item 1: amend agents/qa.md Phase 5 to forbid pytest_collected_ok:true whenever active_tests_count==0, force-include cycle-diff testable files when active set is empty, and add scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py as the canonical executable invariant. Item 2: add scripts/repair-venv.sh that durably restores /root/.claude/venv/bin/python3 by parsing pyvenv.cfg's executable= line (no hardcoded interpreter path) and verifies success via venv/bin/python -m pytest --version exit 0. Both items must ship together because the broken venv is the operational cause of QA's grep-only fallback that produced the vacuous report.
requirement.what: (1) Add a vacuity-rejection invariant + force-include rule + executable guard to QA Phase 5 manifest_verification; (2) repair the missing venv/bin/python3 symlink durably via a script that reads pyvenv.cfg.
requirement.why: QA can currently emit pytest_collected_ok:true with zero active tests — a structurally-allowed greenwash. The prior cycle's hand-correction fixed one report only; the contract that produced it was untouched. Separately, the venv interpreter symlinks are broken, forcing every subagent that invokes pytest to fall back to grep verification, which is the operational driver of the vacuous-report pattern.
where: agents/qa.md (Phase 5 procedural rule around line 1078; manifest_verification schema docblock around lines 1525-1535)

Amend agents/qa.md manifest_verification schema docblock to list vacuous_due_to_empty_active_set and vacuous_reason fields
Create scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py implementing evaluate(dict)->(verdict, reason), exit 0 on ok / ok_vacuous_acknowledged, exit 2 on vacuous_rejected, importable as a Python module

Create scripts/repair-venv.sh that resolves venv via own-script-location + --venv override, parses pyvenv.cfg executable=, creates missing venv/bin/python3 symlink, verifies via pytest --version exit 0, is idempotent (no-op when healthy)

Recreating the venv as a whole

Broader QA agent rewrites or adjacent anti-greenwash rules (codex finding #12)

success_criteria: AC1 (manifest mode): scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py with NO CLI flags exits 2 with verdict:vacuous_rejected when fed the prior cycle's smoking-gun fixture {active_tests_count:0, pytest_collected_ok:true, pytest_failures:[]} on stdin

AC2 (venv plural symlinks + finder + sys.prefix): scripts/repair-venv.sh restores ALL THREE of venv/bin/{python,python3,python3.12} -m pytest --version to exit 0 (regex ^pytest\s+\d+\.\d+); find venv/bin -maxdepth 1 -name 'python*' -type l -xtype l returns empty; venv/bin/python -c 'import sys; assert sys.prefix.endswith("venv")' exits 0; idempotent on second run (prints 'already healthy', mtime unchanged)

AC3 (structural anchors + example replacement + enforcement clause): agents/qa.md carries vacuous_due_to_empty_active_set in BOTH the Phase 5 procedural region (structural anchor: ^#### Phase 5 → ^#{1,4}\s+) AND the manifest_verification JSON object (brace-counted); the JSON object MUST NOT contain {active_tests_count:0} AND {pytest_collected_ok:true} simultaneously; Phase 5 contains an enforcement clause binding exit code 2 → qa.status=fail + primary_cause=qa_oversight + qa.failures[] append
AC4 (manifest-mode regression matrix): guard rejects every vacuous shape (true+empty failures, true+nonempty failures, true+missing failures, null+undeclared vacuity) — all exit 2

AC5 (cycle-diff mode + per-file collection + fail-closed): guard --cycle-diff-files (one test file + one non-test module) --collect-only-cmd 'pytest --collect-only' exits 0 with active_tests_count:1; empty/no-.py diff exits 0 with verdict:ok_vacuous_acknowledged + vacuous_reason containing 'no pytest-collectable files in cycle diff'; one flag without the other or pytest unavailable exits 3 with verdict:guard_blocked
constraints: No hardcoded /usr/bin/python3.12 path in scripts/repair-venv.sh — MUST parse pyvenv.cfg at runtime (codex iter-1 finding #9)
No hardcoded pytest version assertion — AC must match ^pytest\s+\d+\.\d+ (codex iter-1 finding #8)
Guard MANIFEST MODE MUST be pure stdin/stdout/exit-code; no filesystem reads, no subprocess (codex iter-2 BLOCKER #1)

Guard CYCLE-DIFF MODE is opt-in via --cycle-diff-files + --collect-only-cmd; MAY read files and invoke pytest --collect-only; MUST NOT write filesystem; MUST fail closed (exit 3, verdict:guard_blocked) when pytest unavailable or flags incomplete; MUST NEVER fall back to file-name heuristics (codex iter-2 BLOCKER #2)
Cycle-diff mode MUST run collect-only command per file individually (not batched) — pytest exit codes and stdout are per-invocation (codex iter-2 BLOCKER #3)
Force-include rule MUST use git diff as authoritative source; dev-report files_modified[] is advisory only (codex iter-1 finding #2)
Only pytest-collectable test files may raise active_tests_count; non-test implementation files cannot (codex iter-1 finding #3)

Idempotence MUST be based on actual execution health (pytest --version exit 0 for all three symlinks + find -xtype l empty + sys.prefix endswith venv), not symlink presence alone (codex iter-1 finding #10 + QA iter-1 advisory S2)
Repair script MUST NOT mutate site-packages or reinstall packages (codex iter-1 finding #11)

Schema docblock worked example MUST be REPLACED (not appended) with the explicit-vacuous shape; same-JSON-object scope check via brace-counting MUST NOT permit {active_tests_count:0} AND {pytest_collected_ok:true} co-existing (codex iter-2 MAJOR #4 + QA iter-1 blocker B4)

AC3 region locators MUST use structural anchors — Phase 5 region = ^#### Phase 5: → next ^#{1,4}\s+ (NOT ##### sub-subheadings); manifest_verification schema region = brace-counted from "manifest_verification": { → matching } (codex iter-2 MAJOR #5 + QA iter-1 advisory S1)

Phase 5 enforcement clause MUST bind exit code 2 to qa.status=fail + primary_cause=qa_oversight + qa.failures[] append carrying severity:critical + guard verdict/reason — local rule sufficient, no broader verdict-aggregation rewrite (codex iter-2 MAJOR #6 + QA iter-1 blocker B2)

root_cause_analysis.symptom: QA emitted manifest_verification with active_tests_count=0 AND pytest_collected_ok=true AND pytest_failures=[] — a vacuous green report. Separately, every subagent that invokes ~/.claude/venv/bin/python lands on a broken symlink (exit 127).
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: Two coupled defects: (1) agents/qa.md Phase 5 has no invariant gating the report shape when the active set is empty, so a structurally-allowed but semantically-empty 'green' report is permitted; (2) /root/.claude/venv/bin/python3 is missing (the target of both python and python3.12 symlinks), even though pyvenv.cfg's declared executable=/usr/bin/python3.12 exists and pytest is installed in site-packages. The venv defect is the operational reason QA degrades to grep; the QA-contract defect is the reason the degradation passes verification.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — no single commit can be bisected for the symlink (pre-existed in cycle 20260527-054705); QA contract gap is structural (agents/qa.md never carried the invariant).
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: (1) venv: unknown — predates audit; possible cause is partial venv recreation or a prior cleanup script that removed the python3 binary while leaving symlinks. (2) QA contract: Phase 5 was written assuming active_tests_count would always be derived from a populated manifest; the empty-set edge case was never enumerated as a forbidden report shape.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: Together they form a 'lying harness' chain: broken venv -> pytest cannot run -> QA fills the report with vacuous truthy fields -> close gate sees pytest_collected_ok:true and approves. The user has explicitly named this the structural greenwash pattern.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: Symlink known broken at least as of 2026-05-27 (cycle 20260527-054705); contract gap predates that.
observations: venv/bin/python: broken symbolic link to python3 (observed via `file /root/.claude/venv/bin/python` returning 'broken symbolic link to python3')

Both symptoms map back to specific file:line locations in agents/qa.md and to the missing venv/bin/python3 inode.

attempts.proposed_solution: Hand-correct the offending report fields (set pytest_collected_ok:null) and add a real_test_run sidecar plus a correction_note acknowledging the vacuity.
attempts.actual_change: Modified one report file's manifest_verification block; appended sidecar fields. agents/qa.md untouched.
attempts.outcome: That single report became truthful, but agents/qa.md Phase 5 still permits the vacuous shape. The next QA cycle can repeat the pattern.

attempts.proposed_solution: M1-M5 (vacuity invariant + force-include + schema fields + guard script + venv repair); AC1-AC4 verifying invariant, repair, token presence, regression matrix.
attempts.actual_change: Spec text only; artifacts not yet implemented when QA reviewed for spec adequacy.
attempts.outcome: QA rejected with 4 blockers: B1 — M2 force-include rule had no executable AC (AC1/AC4 only tested the vacuity-rejection guard, not the force-include path); B2 — M4 'QA respects guard exit code' was too weak (descriptive, not enforcing — needed exit-2-binds-to-qa.status=fail + primary_cause=qa_oversight + qa.failures[] append); B3 — AC2 only verified venv/bin/python though user wrote 'venv 全 broken symlink' plural (needed all three symlinks tested + broken-symlink finder); B4 — M3 schema edit was append-not-replace, so the smoking-gun shape in the docblock example would survive verbatim with new fields tacked on. Plus advisories S1 (re-anchor AC3 line ranges structurally) and S2 (sys.prefix check).

novelty_check.rationale: vs Attempt 1 (L3 single-report edit): iter-2 still amends the contract that produces reports, not just a report. vs Attempt 2 (iter-1 L4 contract edit): iter-2 (a) makes the force-include rule EXECUTABLE by adding --cycle-diff-files + --collect-only-cmd CLI flags on the guard plus AC5 with synthetic cycle-diff intersection test (B1); (b) strengthens M4 into an ENFORCING procedural rule with concrete failure-attribution (qa.failures[] append carrying severity:critical + primary_cause:qa_oversight, then qa.status=fail) — not a descriptive 'QA respects exit code' line (B2 + codex iter-2 MAJOR #6); (c) EXPANDS AC2 to cover all three symlinks + broken-symlink finder + sys.prefix-endswith-venv check (B3 + S2); (d) REPLACES the schema docblock's worked example outright AND adds a same-JSON-object scope check via brace-counting (no fragile 5-line proximity) (B4 + codex iter-2 MAJOR #4); (e) RE-ANCHORS AC3 to structural markers (Phase 5 H4 boundary via ^#### Phase 5 → ^#{1,4}\s+; manifest_verification JSON object via brace-counting) rather than fragile line ranges (S1 + codex iter-2 MAJOR #5). Iter-2 also splits guard into dual-mode (manifest-mode pure stdin + cycle-diff-mode opt-in CLI) to satisfy codex iter-2 BLOCKER #1, and adds fail-closed exit 3 verdict:guard_blocked when pytest unavailable (codex iter-2 BLOCKER #2), and per-file collection semantics (codex iter-2 BLOCKER #3). The layer is the same L4 as iter-1; the granularity is materially different per QA's blocker list.

development_approach.strategy: Two coupled L4 edits. (1) For QA contract: amend agents/qa.md Phase 5 procedural text to insert the vacuity invariant + force-include rule + guard enforcement clause (exit code 2 → qa.failures[] append with severity:critical+primary_cause:qa_oversight → qa.status=fail); amend the manifest_verification schema docblock to (a) add four new fields (vacuous_due_to_empty_active_set, vacuous_reason, guard_verdict, guard_reason), (b) REPLACE the worked example with the explicit-vacuous shape so the smoking-gun shape no longer appears as the canonical teaching example; create scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py as a dual-mode tool (manifest mode: pure stdin/stdout/exit-code; cycle-diff mode: opt-in via --cycle-diff-files + --collect-only-cmd, per-file collection, fail-closed exit 3 on environment failure). (2) For venv: write scripts/repair-venv.sh that parses pyvenv.cfg, creates the missing venv/bin/python3 symlink, verifies via THREE pytest --version invocations (one per python/python3/python3.12) + find -xtype l empty + sys.prefix-endswith-venv, idempotent — script-location-relative. Add docs/reference/venv-repair.md as discoverability note.
development_approach.validation_approach: AC1 (manifest mode): pipe smoking-gun fixture on stdin (no CLI flags) → exit 2 + verdict:vacuous_rejected; valid non-vacuous fixture → exit 0 + verdict:ok; explicit-vacuous fixture → exit 0 + verdict:ok_vacuous_acknowledged. AC2 (venv repair plural): run scripts/repair-venv.sh on the currently-broken venv; assert ALL THREE of /root/.claude/venv/bin/{python,python3,python3.12} -m pytest --version exit 0 + output matches ^pytest\s+\d+\.\d+; assert `find venv/bin -maxdepth 1 -name 'python*' -type l -xtype l` returns empty; assert `venv/bin/python -c 'import sys; assert sys.prefix.endswith("venv")'` exits 0; assert second run is idempotent (mtime preserved, 'already healthy' printed); assert no hardcoded /usr/bin/python3.NN path outside comments/pyvenv.cfg context. AC3 (structural anchors): static inspection of agents/qa.md confirms vacuous_due_to_empty_active_set appears in BOTH Phase 5 procedural region (^#### Phase 5: → ^#{1,4}\s+ — NOT ##### subheadings) AND manifest_verification JSON object (brace-counted from "manifest_verification": { → matching }); the JSON object MUST NOT contain {active_tests_count:0} + {pytest_collected_ok:true} simultaneously; Phase 5 region contains the forbid phrase (active_tests_count==0 ... MUST NOT/forbid/禁止 ... pytest_collected_ok), the guard script reference (scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py), and the enforcement clause (exit code 2 ... qa.status ... fail + primary_cause within 200 chars of qa_oversight + qa.failures within 600 chars of exit code 2). AC4 (manifest-mode regression matrix): four vacuous shapes each → exit 2 + verdict:vacuous_rejected. AC5 (cycle-diff mode): synthetic diff of one collectable test + one non-test module → exit 0 + active_tests_count:1; empty/no-.py diff → exit 0 + verdict:ok_vacuous_acknowledged + vacuous_reason 'no pytest-collectable files in cycle diff'; one flag missing → exit 3 + verdict:guard_blocked; pytest binary not found → exit 3 + verdict:guard_blocked.

iterations.feedback_summary: 12 findings, all with PROPOSED_FIX. Most impactful: (#1) tighten invariant to forbid pytest_collected_ok:true whenever active_tests_count==0 regardless of failures; (#2) use git diff as authoritative changed-file source; (#3) only pytest-collectable files raise active_tests_count; (#4) AC1 must feed the exact smoking-gun fixture; (#5) extract guard to scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py referenced from qa.md; (#7) AC3 needs presence-in-both-regions; (#8) AC2 must not hardcode pytest version; (#9) venv repair must parse pyvenv.cfg; (#10) idempotence based on execution health; (#11) limit repair to symlink only; (#12) explicit scope-creep guard. All applied to iter-1 spec.

8 findings (3 BLOCKER + 3 MAJOR + 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY), all with PROPOSED_FIX or marked OBSERVATION. BLOCKER #1: dual-mode guard contradicts the 'pure stdin/stdout' purity sentence unless reconciled — split into explicit manifest-mode (pure) + cycle-diff-mode (opt-in, read-only, may run pytest). BLOCKER #2: pytest-unavailable must fail closed (exit 3, verdict:guard_blocked) not fall back to heuristic test_*.py. BLOCKER #3: cycle-diff collection semantics underspecified — must run collect-only per file individually (combined-batch parsing is ambiguous because pytest exit codes are per-invocation). MAJOR #4: B4's 'within 5 lines' proximity check is brittle — replace with same-JSON-object scope check via brace-counting. MAJOR #5: Phase 5 region boundary should be next equal-or-higher heading (^#{1,4}\s+), not just next H4 — future H3/H2 between Phase 5 and Phase 6 would overrun. MAJOR #6: M4's enforcement clause needs a concrete report location — exit 2 must append qa.failures[] entry with severity:critical + primary_cause:qa_oversight, then set qa.status:fail; no broader verdict-aggregation rewrite needed. OBSERVATION #7: three-symlink verification is correctly scoped (verification breadth, not repair complexity). OBSERVATION #8: iter-2 does not add new Must-Haves if framed correctly (AC5 is executable coverage for B1/M2, symlink checks are verification of B3 — keep as AC granularity under existing Must-Haves).

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Iter-1: 12 findings, all applied. Iter-2: 8 findings (3 BLOCKER + 3 MAJOR + 2 OBSERVATION_ONLY), all applied or logged. See iterations[] for detail.

findings.issue: Guard too narrow — only forbids true+empty-failures combo
findings.proposed_fix: Final invariant: active_tests_count==0 forbids pytest_collected_ok:true under any circumstances; require null + vacuous_due_to_empty_active_set:true + vacuous_reason.
findings.rationale: Applied to M1 + AC1 + AC4. The original narrower formulation would have let `true + nonempty failures` slip through.

findings.issue: Skirting path via dev-report files_modified[]
findings.proposed_fix: Use git diff as authoritative source; dev-report advisory only.
findings.rationale: Applied to M2.

findings.issue: Python files != pytest-collectable tests; conflating them creates fake coverage
findings.proposed_fix: Only pytest-collectable test files raise active_tests_count; non-test implementation files are coverage observations.
findings.rationale: Applied to M2.

findings.issue: AC1 must feed the exact smoking-gun fixture, not a paraphrased equivalent
findings.rationale: Applied to AC1 first fixture entry.

findings.issue: AC1 risks becoming runtime-shaped grep if guard is test-only
findings.proposed_fix: Make scripts/qa-manifest-guard.py canonical and reference it from agents/qa.md; test that script directly.
findings.rationale: Applied to M4 + AC1's check.kind=subprocess against the canonical script.

findings.issue: pytest_failures:null may break consumers expecting an array
findings.proposed_fix: Keep pytest_failures as array; add pytest_collected_ok:null + vacuous_due_to_empty_active_set + vacuous_reason.
findings.rationale: Applied — schema keeps pytest_failures as array shape; vacuous declaration carried by the new dedicated fields.

findings.issue: AC3 grep count is weak; comments can satisfy it
findings.proposed_fix: Require one occurrence in Phase 5 procedural rule AND one in schema docblock — positional, not total-count.
findings.rationale: Applied to AC3.assertions[0,1]: token_in_procedural_region + token_in_schema_docblock with separate line-range constraints.

findings.issue: Hardcoded pytest 9.0.3 in AC2 is brittle
findings.proposed_fix: Assert exit 0 + output matching ^pytest\s+\d+\.\d+; do not pin version.
findings.rationale: Applied to AC2.steps.pytest_runs.expected_stdout_regex.

findings.issue: Venv durability needs script-location-based targeting, not cwd-based
findings.proposed_fix: Resolve repo root from script's own path; default to <repo>/venv; parse pyvenv.cfg executable=.
findings.rationale: Applied to M5 and AC2's no_hardcoded_target assertion.

findings.issue: Idempotence based on symlink presence is fragile
findings.proposed_fix: Run venv/bin/python -m pytest --version; only print 'already healthy' on exit 0.
findings.rationale: Applied to M5 condition (f).

findings.issue: Repair script should not mutate site-packages or recreate venv
findings.rationale: Applied to Won't Have list.

findings.issue: Scope creep risk if broader QA-test discovery is added
findings.proposed_fix: Keep QA change to the single invariant + minimal force-include needed to enforce it.
findings.rationale: Applied via Won't Have list and via M1-M5 narrow scope.

findings.issue: Iter-2 BLOCKER #1 (Q1): dual-mode guard contradicts the existing 'pure stdin/stdout' constraint unless the spec reconciles it. --cycle-diff-files + --collect-only-cmd is the right shape per B1, but running pytest is not 'pure stdin-only' and introduces filesystem reads + subprocess/env dependency.
findings.proposed_fix: Amend the guard contract to define two explicit modes: manifest mode (stdin-only, pure, no filesystem/subprocess) and cycle-diff mode (opt-in via the two CLI flags, read-only, may run pytest, no filesystem writes). Do not leave the old purity sentence unqualified.
findings.rationale: Applied to M4 and Technical Hints — the guard contract now explicitly splits into manifest mode + cycle-diff mode; manifest mode retains the pure stdin/stdout/exit-code constraint; cycle-diff mode is read-only with explicit pytest-invocation allowance. The old single-sentence purity claim has been replaced with the dual-mode contract.

findings.issue: Iter-2 BLOCKER #2 (Q6): pytest-unavailable behavior must fail closed, not heuristic-fallback. If --collect-only-cmd cannot execute because pytest/venv is broken, treating test_*.py as collectable would recreate the grep-only degradation the requirement is trying to kill.
findings.proposed_fix: Command-not-found, interpreter failure, or unexpected pytest failure emits guard_blocked with nonzero exit (exit 3); manifest_verification.guard_verdict='guard_blocked' and guard_reason explains the failure. Use primary_cause:'environment' unless the failure is a real changed-test collection/import failure.
findings.rationale: Applied to M4 fail-closed clause and AC5 final two cases (one-flag-missing → exit 3; command-not-found → exit 3). Won't Have list now explicitly forbids file-name heuristic fallback. Exit 3 + verdict:guard_blocked is documented.

findings.issue: Iter-2 BLOCKER #3: cycle-diff collectability semantics are underspecified. 'Run collect-only cmd against cycle-diff files and derive subset' is ambiguous for mixed test/non-test files — combined pytest output can miscount which file was collectable. Also .py files with zero collectable tests must be explicitly vacuous, not undefined.
findings.proposed_fix: Define per-file collection: for each .py cycle-diff path, run the collect-only command with that one path; count it only if pytest collects >=1 test. Treat pytest exit 5 / no tests as non-collectable. If final count is 0, emit ok_vacuous_acknowledged with reason 'no pytest-collectable files in cycle diff'.
findings.rationale: Applied to M4 cycle-diff mode contract and AC5 per-file semantics. Constraint added: 'cycle-diff mode runs collect-only per file individually — combined-batch parsing is forbidden.' Pytest exit 5 is explicitly mapped to non-collectable. The 0-count case emits ok_vacuous_acknowledged with the literal vacuous_reason string.

findings.issue: Iter-2 MAJOR #4 (Q2): the 'within 5 lines' B4 check is brittle. It can miss the bad example if formatting/comments push keys apart, and it can false-positive across adjacent examples.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace proximity check with same-object-scope check: extract the manifest_verification JSON object by brace counting from "manifest_verification": { to its matching }, then fail if that object contains both "active_tests_count": 0 and "pytest_collected_ok": true.
findings.rationale: Applied to AC3 assertion no_smoking_gun_example — now uses brace-counted same-JSON-object scope check rather than a fragile 5-line proximity. M3 also explicitly mandates example REPLACEMENT (not append) with the explicit-vacuous shape as the new worked example.

findings.issue: Iter-2 MAJOR #5 (Q3): Phase 5 region boundary should be next equal-or-higher heading, not only next H4. Current agents/qa.md happens to have #### Phase 6 next, but a future ### or ## before the next #### would make the verifier overrun.
findings.proposed_fix: Start at ^#### Phase 5: and end at the first later heading with level <=4: regex ^#{1,4}\s+. Do not end on ##### subheadings.
findings.rationale: Applied to AC3 token_in_procedural_region anchor: '^#### Phase 5: Manifest Verification → ^#{1,4}\s+ (NOT including ##### sub-subheadings)'. Explicitly noted in the Edge Cases & Risks section.

findings.issue: Iter-2 MAJOR #6 (Q5): primary_cause:qa_oversight needs a concrete report location. Adding guard_verdict/guard_reason alone records the guard result but does not structurally create the required failure attribution.
findings.proposed_fix: In Phase 5 text, require that guard exit 2 appends a qa.failures[] entry with severity:'critical' and primary_cause:'qa_oversight', then sets qa.status:'fail' regardless of other outcomes. No broad verdict-aggregation rewrite needed if this local rule is explicit.
findings.rationale: Applied to M4 enforcement contract clause and AC3 assertion guard_enforcement_clause. Phase 5 now contains the literal enforcement rule binding exit code 2 → qa.failures[] append (severity:critical + primary_cause:qa_oversight) → qa.status=fail. Won't Have list excludes broader verdict-aggregation rewrite (local rule is sufficient).

findings.issue: Iter-2 OBSERVATION_ONLY (Q4): three-symlink verification is correctly scoped. This is verification breadth, not repair scope creep. Root cause may be one missing venv/bin/python3, but the user-relevant surfaces are python, python3, and python3.12.
findings.rationale: Logged in codex_consult.findings; no spec change needed — AC2 already tests all three symlinks plus the broken-symlink finder. The framing 'verification breadth in scope, repair complexity not' is reflected in the Evidence pair 2 narrative and the M5 step (e) verification list.

findings.issue: Iter-2 OBSERVATION_ONLY (Q7): iter-2 does not inherently add new Must-Haves if framed correctly. AC5 is executable coverage for B1/M2, not a new product requirement. The symlink checks are verification of B3. Keep these as AC granularity under existing Must-Haves, not new scope.
findings.rationale: Logged in codex_consult.findings; confirms iter-2 stays within M1-M5 boundaries. Won't Have list remains intact with no Must-Have additions. AC5 is framed as executable coverage of M2's force-include rule.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 12 iter-1 codex findings applied (see iter:1 entries). All 6 actionable iter-2 codex findings (3 BLOCKER + 3 MAJOR) applied: dual-mode guard contract (BLOCKER #1 → M4 + Technical Hints); fail-closed environment handling (BLOCKER #2 → M4 + AC5 + Won't Have); per-file collection semantics (BLOCKER #3 → M4 + AC5); same-JSON-object scope check via brace-counting (MAJOR #4 → AC3 no_smoking_gun_example); structural Phase 5 region boundary using next ^#{1,4}\s+ heading (MAJOR #5 → AC3 token_in_procedural_region); concrete qa.failures[] append + qa.status=fail enforcement clause (MAJOR #6 → M4 + AC3 guard_enforcement_clause). Two iter-2 OBSERVATION_ONLY findings (Q4 three-symlink scoping, Q7 no new Must-Haves) logged with no spec change required — these confirmed existing iter-2 design.
